task_completion.description: The personal finance spreadsheet is created and contains the purchased items data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet application (e.g., LibreOffice Calc) is open showing a list of purchased items (e.g., 'Home Exercise Bike', 'Camping Tent', 'Instant Pot') with their prices and usage counts.

task_completion.description: The spreadsheet calculates the cost-per-use for the items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dedicated column exists showing the calculated cost-per-use (Price divided by Times Used) for the items.

task_completion.description: Potential division-by-zero errors are handled gracefully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Items with zero usage (specifically 'Camping Tent' and 'Instant Pot') do NOT display a '#DIV/0!' error in the cost-per-use column. They should show a handled value (e.g., blank, a text warning, or the full price).

task_completion.description: Conditional logic is applied for value assessment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A column exists that categorizes the value of each purchase (e.g., 'Excellent', 'Good', 'Poor', 'Waste') based on the calculated cost-per-use.

task_completion.description: The spreadsheet is sorted to identify poor-value purchases.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The data rows are sorted by the cost-per-use column in descending order, meaning the items with the highest cost-per-use (or zero usage/Waste items) appear at the top of the list.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical spreadsheet application to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a GUI spreadsheet application (like LibreOffice Calc) being actively used, rather than just a terminal window or a basic text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a terminal script to bypass GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent writing and executing a Python/Bash script (e.g., using pandas or openpyxl) in the terminal to generate the final .xlsx file directly.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely interacted with the spreadsheet interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Visual cues of interaction such as selected cells, visible formula bars containing IF/IFERROR statements, or open sorting/formatting dialog boxes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The spreadsheet contains 12 items. Two items ('Camping Tent' and 'Instant Pot') have 0 usage and must NOT show #DIV/0! errors. Value assessment thresholds are: Excellent (<$1), Good (<$5), Poor (<$20), Waste (>=$20 or never used). The final data must be sorted by cost-per-use in descending order (highest cost-per-use at the top).
